name: Chocodeath Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9x13 inch pan. Set aside.
2. Sift together the cake flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
4. Blend in the eggs and beat until well combined. Stir in the vanilla extract and melted chocolate.
5. Alternately add the dry ingredients and sour cream to the butter mixture, beating well after each addition.
6. Stir in the boiling water. The batter will be thin. Divide the cake batter into two portions.
7. Pour one portion into the prepared pan and bake for 10-12 minutes or until firm to touch. Remove from the oven.
8. Meanwhile, prepare the filling by mixing the butter, caramels, and sweetened condensed milk in a saucepan over low heat, stirring often. It can also be melted in the microwave.
9. Pour the caramel mixture over the baked cake portion. Return the pan to the oven.
10. Bake for an additional 15-20 minutes or until firm to touch (it took me 25 minutes). Allow to cool before frosting or serving with whipped cream or a la mode.
